title: Althaea minoricensis
intro:
There is no information found on a plant called Althaea minoricensis. However, I can provide you with information on Althaea officinalis, also known as the marshmallow plant.

Althaea officinalis, commonly referred to as the marshmallow plant, is a perennial herb native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including soothing digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and providing relief from coughs and sore throats. The bioactive constituents of Althaea officinalis include mucilages, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include decoctions, infusions, and topical creams, often used to treat skin irritations and wounds.
uses: Althaea minoricensis, also known as the lesser marsh mallow, has been used in traditional medicine for its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's roots and leaves have been used to make teas, infusions, and poultices to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and skin irritations. Its high mucilage content makes it an effective natural remedy for soothing sore throats, coughs, and stomach ulcers. In traditional medicine, Althaea minoricensis has also been used to treat fever, bronchitis, and other respiratory conditions. Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of Althaea minoricensis for these medicinal uses.
